sections.title: Server time (same wall-clock for all servers)

cases.title: Timespan, different dates
cases.description_before: Combat Drills for Tangtang is available from
cases.note: Inline text shows "(server)" once at the end. Tooltip shows your local time per server.

cases.title: Single time
cases.description_before: The Red Knight becomes available at
cases.description_after: , then you'll have 72 hours to challenge her before she retreats.

sections.title: Absolute time (same real-world moment for all)

cases.title: Maintenance window (UTC+8)
cases.description_after: . All game services will be unavailable during this window.
cases.note: Inline shows your local time with your TZ once at end of range. Tooltip shows each server's wall-clock.

cases.description_before: Event begins after maintenance, runs until server reset:
cases.note: Start gets your TZ (EDT), end gets "(server)" so both labels are shown.

cases.title: Raw text with start and end
cases.note: Inline text stays exactly as provided. Tooltip should still be built from the datetime parameters.

cases.title: Raw text + single time + server filter
cases.note: Inline text should remain raw, and the tooltip should be limited to the specified server.

cases.title: Cross-year range (Dec 2025 - Jan 2026)
cases.description_after: . Special decorations and limited quests are available across all regions during this period.
cases.note: Year shown on both because they differ.

cases.title: Server time — single moment
cases.note: No timezone offset. JS converts to the viewer's local wall-clock time per server.

cases.title: Absolute timespan
cases.note: UTC+8 offset anchors a real-world moment. JS converts to the viewer's local time and shows it per server. An absolute timespan is often a maintenance window affecting all servers at once.

cases.title: Raw text override
cases.note: Inline text stays exactly as written. The tooltip is still built from the datetime parameters.

cases.title: Soft error — unparseable date
cases.note: Start is present but cannot be parsed. The raw input is shown as-is so the page is still readable. Adds a MediaWiki error tracking category.

cases.title: Hard error — no start time
cases.note: Missing required parameter. Shown in red and tracked in the error category.

cases.title: Filtered to Americas, mixed definitions
cases.note: Start is absolute (UTC-5); end is server-relative with no offset. The filter limits the tooltip to the Americas server. Both TZ labels appear inline because the types differ.

cases.title: Same-day in source TZ
cases.note: The range is one calendar day in China (UTC+8) but viewers in the Americas see it cross into the next local date.

cases.title: Crosses midnight in source TZ
cases.note: The range spans two calendar days in China. One calendar day in Americas.

cases.title: US DST ends during the displayed span
cases.note: In a U.S. timezone that observes DST, the localized inline output may need different timezone labels on each side of the span, such as EDT then EST.
